title: AO Farms Dashboard UI — Phase 1 Shell

requirements.summary: Implement a top navigation bar with the AO Farms logo on the left and navigation links routing to existing app routes, with active link highlighted in dark green pill style
acceptanceCriteria: Logo displays on the top-left with green cow icon and 'A.O. Farms' text
All 6 nav links are visible on desktop
Active route link is highlighted with a dark green filled style
Navigation links route to their respective pages

file_operations.description: Replace the existing navigation bar implementation with a new one that displays the AO Farms logo (using frontend/public/assets/generated/ao-farms-logo.dim_120x80.png) on the left, followed by six navigation links (Dashboard, Cattle, Milk Production, Inventory, Customers, Deliveries) that route to their respective TanStack Router paths (/dashboard, /cattle, /milk-production, /inventory, /customers, /deliveries). Apply a filled dark-green pill/button style (using CSS custom property --farm-primary or #1a5c2a) to the active link. Ensure the nav bar is sticky at the top, uses the existing desktop and mobile layout structure, and integrates with TanStack Router's Link component for active state detection. Verify the generated logo image dimensions and reference it correctly.

requirements.summary: Add a full-width hero banner below the nav bar on the Dashboard page showing an illustrated farm landscape with overlaid welcome text
acceptanceCriteria: Hero banner spans full width of the page
Farm landscape illustration is rendered as the banner background
Title 'Welcome to AO Farms' and subtitle text are visible over the banner
A cow emoji or small cow icon appears next to the title

file_operations.description: Add a full-width hero banner section immediately below the Layout component's navigation bar. Use the generated farm landscape illustration frontend/public/assets/generated/farm-hero-banner.dim_1400x300.png as the background image. Overlay the text 'Welcome to AO Farms' (with a cow emoji 🐄 next to it) and the subtitle 'Here's your dairy farm overview for today' on the left side of the banner. Style the banner to span the full width of the page, ensure text is readable over the background (use a semi-transparent overlay or text shadow if needed), and match the farm-themed color palette. Verify the generated image path and dimensions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Display 6 summary stat cards on the Dashboard in a single row showing cattle count, milk production, inventory, low stock, deliveries, and missed deliveries with icons and subtext
acceptanceCriteria: All 6 stat cards are rendered in a responsive row
Each card shows label, value, subtext, and icon
Values are pulled from backend query hooks (cattle count, milk records, inventory, deliveries)
Low stock threshold is 10
Cards use cream/beige background matching farm theme

file_operations.description: Below the hero banner, add a responsive grid/row of 6 summary stat cards. Each card should display: (1) Total Cattle — total count with subtext showing active count from getAllCattle(); (2) Today's Milk — total litres from getAllMilkProductionRecords() filtered to today; (3) Inventory Items — total unique items from getAllInventoryItems(); (4) Low Stock — items with quantity below threshold 10 from getLowStockItems(); (5) Today's Deliveries — orders with Delivered status today from getAllOrders(); (6) Missed Deliveries — orders not delivered today from getAllOrders(). Use existing admin query hooks from useAdminQueries. Each card should have a label, numeric value, subtext, and a small icon (use Lucide React icons or emoji). Apply a light beige/cream background (CSS custom property --background or #f5f0e8) and green icon accents (--farm-primary or #1a5c2a). Ensure responsive layout (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-6 or similar).

requirements.summary: Add a 7-Day Milk Production line/area chart on the Dashboard showing daily milk totals for the last 7 days
acceptanceCriteria: Chart renders in the lower-left panel of the Dashboard
X-axis shows last 7 days
Y-axis shows milk volume in litres
Data comes from milk production records

file_operations.description: Below the stat cards row, add a two-column layout (left and right panels). In the left panel, render the existing MilkProductionTrendChart component (frontend/src/components/MilkProductionTrendChart.tsx) with the title '7-Day Milk Production'. Pass the last 7 days of milk production records from getAllMilkProductionRecords() filtered and aggregated by date. The chart should display daily totals with date on the X-axis and litres on the Y-axis. Ensure the panel has a card-style background (warm white) and appropriate padding. Use the existing MilkProductionTrendChart component without modification.

requirements.summary: Add a Recent Milk Entries panel on the Dashboard listing the most recent milk production log entries with an empty state
acceptanceCriteria: Panel appears in the lower-right of the Dashboard
Lists recent milk entries with cattle tag, date, and litres
Shows empty state message when no records exist
Displays at most 5–8 most recent entries

file_operations.description: In the right panel of the two-column layout (next to the 7-Day Milk Production chart), add a 'Recent Milk Entries' section. Fetch milk production records from getAllMilkProductionRecords(), sort by date descending, and display the 5–8 most recent entries. Each entry should show the cattle tag, date (formatted), and volume in litres. If no records exist, display an empty state message like 'No milk production records yet'. Use a card-style background (warm white) and list or table layout. Ensure responsive sizing and match the farm theme.

requirements.summary: Apply a consistent farm-themed visual style across all pages using beige/cream background, dark green primary color, and warm white cards
acceptanceCriteria: Page background is beige/cream
Navigation bar uses dark green background
Primary buttons and active states use dark green
Cards have warm white background
Typography uses earthy/dark tones

file_operations.description: Update the existing CSS custom properties in the :root selector to enforce the farm-themed visual style. Set --background to a beige/cream color (#f5f0e8 or similar), --farm-primary to dark green (#1a5c2a or similar), --card to warm white, and adjust foreground/text colors to earthy/dark tones. Ensure these variables are used consistently across light and dark mode definitions. Do not create new global stylesheet paths; use the existing frontend/src/index.css entry point.

Update the Tailwind color palette to align with the farm theme. Ensure 'farm' color tokens (farm-primary, farm-background, farm-accent) map to the appropriate OKLCH values for beige/cream backgrounds and dark green accents. Verify that the existing 'background', 'card', 'primary', and 'foreground' tokens in the theme.extend.colors section reflect the farm color scheme. No new color tokens are required if the existing ones can be reused.

Ensure the navigation bar background uses the dark green primary color (bg-farm-primary or bg-[#1a5c2a]) and that the main page content area uses the beige/cream background (bg-background or bg-[#f5f0e8]). Apply consistent padding and spacing to match the farm theme. This change should be integrated with the REQ-1 navigation bar update.
